skill.name: canvas-design
skill.description: Creates design-forward poster, art, and static canvas outputs as PNG or PDF files using a generated visual philosophy.
skill.summary: Generate polished visual design concepts and express them as static PNG or PDF canvas artwork.
skill.author: ArtemisAI

security_audit.summary: Most static findings are false positives caused by standard Apache and SIL Open Font License text, plus Markdown backticks around a local font path. The skill also contains a prompt injection-style instruction that fabricates prior user feedback and an instruction to download arbitrary fonts, so it should not be published without remediation.

critical_findings.title: Prompt Injection Attempt Detected
critical_findings.description: The skill states that the user already requested a pristine museum-quality masterpiece. This fabricates user feedback inside the skill instructions and can override the actual user request during execution.
critical_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line explicitly claims a prior user statement that may not exist. This is a direct attempt to insert false user intent into the model context.

medium_findings.title: Unbounded External Font Download Instruction
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to download whatever fonts are needed. This can cause network access to arbitrary font sources and introduce untrusted binary assets into generated work.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instruction clearly authorizes external downloads, but it does not name a destination or show exfiltration. The risk is supply-chain exposure rather than confirmed malware.

low_findings.title: License Text Static Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL, weak cryptographic algorithm, and reconnaissance matches in font license files and LICENSE.txt are standard license terms and reference URLs. They are not executable code and do not perform network access, cryptography, or system inspection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are plain license documents. They contain legal wording and public license URLs, with no executable behavior.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Are Not Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The external command finding at SKILL.md line 108 is caused by Markdown backticks around the local path ./canvas-fonts. It is not Ruby or shell command execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The backticks delimit inline Markdown code around a directory path. There is no shell syntax, interpreter invocation, or user-controlled command string.

dangerous_patterns.title: Prompt Injection Attempt Detected
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill states that the user already requested a pristine museum-quality masterpiece. This fabricates user feedback inside the skill instructions and can override the actual user request during execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The line explicitly claims a prior user statement that may not exist. This is a direct attempt to insert false user intent into the model context.

dangerous_patterns.title: Unbounded External Font Download Instruction
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill instructs the agent to download whatever fonts are needed. This can cause network access to arbitrary font sources and introduce untrusted binary assets into generated work.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The instruction clearly authorizes external downloads, but it does not name a destination or show exfiltration. The risk is supply-chain exposure rather than confirmed malware.

content.value_statement: Static visual design often needs a clear art direction before production. This skill creates a visual philosophy and turns it into polished PNG or PDF canvas artwork.
actual_capabilities: Creates a concise visual philosophy before producing the final artwork.
Guides poster, art print, and static design composition for PNG or PDF output.
Emphasizes typography, spacing, color, form, and visual hierarchy.
Uses bundled font files from the canvas-fonts directory when appropriate.
Supports single-page output and multi-page visual series when requested.
limitations: It provides design instructions, not a standalone rendering engine.
Final quality depends on the host AI tool and available file-generation tools.
The current version contains unsafe instruction patterns that require review before publication.
It is focused on static artwork, not interactive interfaces or animation.

use_cases.title: Produce Campaign Posters
use_cases.description: Create a design philosophy and final static artwork for event posters, announcements, or cultural campaigns.

use_cases.title: Develop Editorial Art
use_cases.description: Turn an article theme, book concept, or magazine feature into a visual-first PNG or PDF composition.

use_cases.title: Explore Brand Visual Directions
use_cases.description: Generate an aesthetic direction with typography, color, and spatial rules for early identity exploration.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Simple Poster
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for one static design.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a one-page poster for a community workshop about sustainable repair. Use minimal text and export a PNG.

prompt_templates.title: Build an Editorial Artwork
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request with a clear subject and tone.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a design philosophy and a single PDF artwork for an essay about quiet technology in public spaces.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Visual Identity Study
prompt_templates.prompt: Develop a visual philosophy for a small architecture studio, then create a refined static canvas showing the direction.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Multi-Page Art Series
prompt_templates.prompt: Create three related pages for a coffee-table art series about memory, maps, and urban night light. Keep text sparse.

output_examples.input: Poster for an experimental jazz night with a restrained but intense mood.
output_examples.output: A short design philosophy named around disciplined improvisation, followed by a one-page PNG poster with sparse typography, structured marks, and a limited palette.

output_examples.input: Editorial artwork for an article about climate data and personal memory.
output_examples.output: A PDF artwork that uses layered grids, subtle reference markers, and small labels to express the subject visually.

output_examples.input: Visual direction for a ceramic studio launch.
output_examples.output: A quiet brand-art composition with paper-like texture, careful spacing, and minimal text integrated into the canvas.

best_practices: Give the subject, audience, output format, and any required text before generation starts.
Provide brand colors, dimensions, and accessibility needs when they matter.
Review the design philosophy before final output when accuracy is more important than speed.
anti_patterns: Do not use it for text-heavy documents that need detailed reading flow.
Do not request imitation of living artists or copyrighted visual styles.
Do not allow unrestricted external asset downloads in controlled environments.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates a design philosophy and uses it to guide static PNG or PDF visual artwork.

faq.question: Does it create interactive designs?
faq.answer: No. It is focused on static canvas artwork, posters, and PDF pages.

faq.question: Can it use custom fonts?
faq.answer: It can reference bundled fonts, but external font downloads should be restricted or reviewed.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish now?
faq.answer: No. The audit found a prompt injection-style instruction that should be removed first.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What input should users provide?
faq.answer: Users should provide the subject, audience, mood, output format, and any required text.
